title: tanacetum maximum
intro: Tanacetum maximum, also known as Feverfew, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches and migraines, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Feverfew include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and even prevent the formation of blood clots. Herbal preparations of Feverfew can be found in the form of teas, capsules, and tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, headaches, and menstrual cramps.
